scenes.text: Introduction to Docker: Docker is a platform designed to help developers create, deploy, and run applications inside lightweight containers. It's revolutionizing software development by solving application delivery challenges.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Developer] --> B[Docker Platform] --> C[Container]; C --> D[Application]

scenes.text: Understanding Containers: Containers encapsulate an application with all its dependencies, ensuring consistent environments across different systems.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Application] --> B[Dependencies]; B --> C[Container]

scenes.text: Docker Architecture Overview: Docker consists of components like Docker Client, Docker Daemon, Docker Engine, Containers, and Docker Hub.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Docker Client] --> B[Docker Daemon]; B --> C[Docker Engine]; C --> D[Containers]; B --> E[Docker Hub]

scenes.text: Docker Installation: Installing Docker involves downloading the Docker software suitable for your operating system from the official Docker website.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Operating System] --> B[Docker Installation]; B --> C[Docker Software]

scenes.text: Building Docker Images: Images are templates for Docker containers. We build them using Dockerfiles that describe the steps needed to setup the environment.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Dockerfile] --> B[Build Process]; B --> C[Docker Image]

scenes.text: Running Docker Containers: Containers are instances of Docker images. They run applications with specified configurations, utilizing isolated resources.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Docker Image] --> B[Run Command]; B --> C[Running Container]

scenes.text: Docker Networking: Docker provides several networking modes (bridge, host, overlay) to facilitate communication between containers.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Container1] -- Bridge --> B[Network]; B -- Overlay --> C[Container2]

scenes.text: Docker Storage Management: Docker volumes and bind mounts help manage persistent data separately from the container's filesystem.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Storage] --> B[Volumes]; B --> C[Containers]

scenes.text: Docker Compose: Docker Compose is a tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications through the use of a YAML file.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Yaml File] --> B[Docker Compose]; B --> C[Multiple Containers]

scenes.text: Scaling with Docker Swarm: Docker Swarm allows us to orchestrate and manage clusters of Docker nodes, providing high-availability and scaling capabilities.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Node1] --> B[Swarm Cluster]; A --> C[Node2]; B --> D[Scaling Services]
